title: Rich Russian Pork Braise
description: A hearty, comforting Russian braise made with pork, lemongrass, and shio-koji cure, perfect for a cozy dinner.
instructions: Season the pork shoulder with salt, pepper, and the shio-koji cure, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it's softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the lemongrass, caraway seeds, allspice, and bay leaves to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the beef broth, red wine (if using), brown sugar, and apple cider vinegar to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until it's tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the braising liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Skim any excess fat from the surface of the braising liquid and season it with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the pork with the braising liquid spooned over the top.
